description: A highly contagious viral infection of the respiratory passages causing fever, severe aching, and catarrh.
home_care_advice: Rest in bed, drink plenty of water, keep warm, and take over-the-counter pain relievers if appropriate. Monitor temperature.
medical_references: WHO Guidelines on Seasonal Influenza Prevention

description: An infectious disease caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus, affecting the respiratory system with varied severity.
home_care_advice: Self-isolate, rest, stay hydrated, monitor blood oxygen saturation (SpO2), and seek medical care if breathing becomes difficult.
medical_references: WHO Therapeutics and COVID-19 Living Guideline
CDC Clinical Care for COVID-19 Patients

description: A mild, self-limiting viral infection of the upper respiratory tract.
home_care_advice: Stay hydrated, rest, use saline nasal sprays, and rinse throat with warm salt water.
medical_references: CDC Common Colds: Protect Yourself and Others

description: A neurological condition characterized by recurrent, intense, throbbing headaches, often unilateral and accompanied by sensory sensitivity.
home_care_advice: Rest in a quiet, dark room. Apply a cool compress to the forehead or neck. Stay hydrated and avoid known triggers (caffeine, bright lights).
red_flags: headache with fever and stiff neck

description: Sudden inflammation or irritation of the lining of the stomach, often causing burning or indigestion.
home_care_advice: Eat small, frequent, bland meals. Avoid spicy, fatty, acidic foods, alcohol, and caffeine. Do not lie down immediately after eating.

description: Chest discomfort or pain caused by reduced blood flow to the heart muscle, potentially indicating a heart attack.
home_care_advice: Sit down immediately and rest. Do not exert yourself. Seek immediate emergency medical assistance.
medical_references: CDC Heart Attack Symptoms and Emergency Response
red_flags: chest pain lasting more than 5 minutes

description: An acute worsening of asthma symptoms, causing narrowing of airways and respiratory distress.
home_care_advice: Use your quick-relief inhaler (rescue bronchodilator) immediately. Sit upright, remain calm, and seek medical help if breathing does not improve.
medical_references: Global Initiative for Asthma (GINA) Pocket Guide
red_flags: inability to speak in full sentences

description: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines, typically caused by a viral or bacterial infection, leading to vomiting and diarrhea.
home_care_advice: Sip Oral Rehydration Salts (ORS) or water frequently. Eat a bland diet (bananas, rice, applesauce). Avoid dairy and fatty foods.
red_flags: inability to keep liquids down for 24 hours

description: An allergic reaction or localized inflammation of the skin caused by contact with a specific substance.
home_care_advice: Wash the area with cool water. Apply calamine lotion or cool compresses. Avoid scratching or contact with potential allergens.

description: A medical emergency caused by interrupted blood supply to the brain, characterized by rapid onset neurological deficits.
home_care_advice: Act F.A.S.T. (Face drooping, Arm weakness, Speech difficulty, Time to call emergency). Lie down on side and seek immediate emergency care.
medical_references: CDC Stroke Prevention and Signs Reference
red_flags: sudden loss of vision or coordination

description: A severe, potentially life-threatening allergic reaction that can occur rapidly after exposure to an allergen.
home_care_advice: Administer epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen) if available and prescribed. Seek emergency services immediately.

description: An infection in any part of the urinary system, commonly the bladder, causing pain and frequent urination.
home_care_advice: Drink plenty of water to flush out bacteria. Avoid bladder irritants like alcohol and caffeine. Consult a doctor for diagnostic confirmation.
red_flags: pain in lower back or side (kidney pain)

description: A mild to moderate pain that is often described as feeling like a tight band around the head.
home_care_advice: Practice relaxation techniques, gentle neck stretching, and manage stress. Ensure adequate sleep and hydration.

description: Inflammation of the lining of the bronchial tubes, often following a cold or respiratory infection.
home_care_advice: Use a humidifier, drink warm fluids, avoid smoke/pollutants, and rest. Use cough lozenges or honey to soothe throat.

description: Acute inflammation of the appendix, requiring urgent surgical intervention.
home_care_advice: Do not eat, drink, or take pain relievers (which can mask symptoms). Seek emergency surgical assessment immediately.
red_flags: sharp, worsening pain in right lower abdomen
inability to walk due to pain

description: A serious and sometimes fatal disease caused by a parasite that commonly infects a certain type of mosquito which feeds on humans, resulting in recurring high fever, chills, and flu-like illness.
home_care_advice: Seek immediate medical evaluation for diagnosis (blood test) and antimalarial prescription. Rest, stay hydrated, and monitor temperature.

description: A mosquito-borne viral disease causing sudden high fever, severe headaches, retro-orbital pain, skin rash, and intense joint and muscle pain.
home_care_advice: Rest, stay hydrated with fluids containing electrolytes, and take acetaminophen/paracetamol for pain/fever. Avoid NSAIDs (aspirin, ibuprofen) as they increase bleeding risk.
medical_references: WHO Dengue and Severe Dengue Guidelines

description: A life-threatening bacterial infection caused by Salmonella Typhi, characterized by sustained high fever, weakness, abdominal pain, headache, and sometimes a rash.
home_care_advice: Requires prompt antibiotic treatment prescribed by a doctor. Drink clean boiled water, eat easily digestible foods, and maintain strict personal hygiene.
medical_references: WHO Typhoid Vaccination and Control Guidelines
CDC Typhoid Fever Information for Clinicians
